highlighted.tagline: New DOM structure with wrapper and portal
highlighted.whyItMatters: Handsontable now renders inside a wrapper element and routes overlays through a portal. Stylesheets that depended on the previous DOM tree need updates, and any code that walks the rendered table from the outside must be reviewed.

Several theme CSS variables are renamed and new --ht-radio-* variables are added. Backward compatibility shims keep most pages rendering, but you should migrate to the new names to avoid future removal.

The modifyData hook now receives visual row and column indexes instead of physical ones. Translate any cached physical indexes to visual coordinates inside your handler, or use toPhysicalRow / toPhysicalColumn for storage lookups.

highlighted.tagline: Custom borders no longer overlap row headers
highlighted.whyItMatters: Custom borders now stay inside the data area and no longer paint over the row header. Layouts that relied on bordered cells bleeding into the header column will look different - adjust by widening row headers or moving the border one cell inward.

A standalone-component Angular wrapper ships under @handsontable/angular-wrapper. The legacy @handsontable/angular package keeps working but is now in maintenance mode - new Angular apps should adopt the new wrapper.
